title: Catamaran Charter Wedding in Marbella: Costs and Tips 2026
html:
A catamaran charter wedding in Marbella sets your ceremony on a stable twin-hull boat leaving Puerto Banús marina, with day prices typically from EUR 1,200 to EUR 4,500 depending on guests and season. BoatHire24 lists verified catamarans and crew across the Costa del Sol.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most wedding charters leave from a few well-known marinas and bays, each an easy transfer from Marbella town. The water close to shore is sheltered, which keeps a flat deck steady for vows.

  • Puerto Banús marina: the main luxury harbour, around 7 kilometres west of Marbella, and the usual departure point for larger catamarans.
  • Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo): the town's own harbour, handy for seafront guests.
  • Puerto Cabopino: a smaller, quieter marina east of town.
  • La Bajadilla (Puerto Pesquero): the working fishing port, used by some local operators.
  • Estepona marina: a short cruise west, often paired with sunset returns.
  • Cabopino bay: a calm anchorage for swimming and drinks between ceremony and dinner.

Which catamaran or yacht should you charter for a Marbella wedding?

For a wedding, choose a wide-beam sailing or power catamaran of 40 to 53 feet, which seats 10 to 30 guests on a level deck. A catamaran gives flat space for a ceremony, a shaded saloon and gentle motion that suits older guests. The range below covers the choices.

  1. Power catamaran (40 to 45 ft): intimate ceremonies and toasts, seats 10 to 16 guests, from EUR 1,200 a day.
  2. Sailing catamaran (45 to 53 ft): larger receptions with a buffet and dancing, seats up to 30 guests, from EUR 1,800 a day.
  3. Motor yacht: for couples who want speed and a sleek profile rather than a twin hull, you can compare the Sunseeker Predator 58, a sharp flybridge boat that carries a smaller wedding party in style, from EUR 2,500 a day.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht (over 60 ft): full-day celebrations with catering and a hostess, seats up to 12 cruising guests, from EUR 4,000 a day.
  5. Glass-fronted day catamaran: drinks receptions and dolphin spotting, seats 20 to 40 standing guests, from EUR 35 per head shared.

How much does a catamaran wedding charter cost in Marbella?

Prices move with boat size, crew, season and any catering. As a 2026 guide, the bands below cover the common options, and you can book a boat online once your date and headcount are set.

  • A shared catamaran sailing with drinks runs from about EUR 35 to EUR 60 per person.
  • A small private motorboat for a half-day starts from roughly EUR 450.
  • A full-day private catamaran for a wedding party costs from about EUR 1,800 to EUR 3,500.
  • A crewed yacht with a hostess and catering typically runs from EUR 4,000 upward.

How does booking a wedding charter actually work?

You pick a boat and date, confirm guest numbers and any catering, then pay a deposit to hold the charter. Wedding bookings need more lead time than a casual day out, because florists, a celebrant and catering must align with the boat. To compare layouts and capacities, you can search available boats by date and headcount.

Most Marbella operators confirm the meeting marina, the included hours and whether fuel, a skipper and soft drinks are in the price. Read the cancellation terms carefully, since wedding charters often carry stricter deposit rules.

When is the best season for a wedding charter in Marbella?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm, settled weather and long evenings make sunset ceremonies reliable. May and October are quieter and cooler, with softer light that photographers prefer. Local winds shape the day more than the calendar does.

In Marbella the Levante wind from the east can build a short chop by mid-afternoon, so couples often set the ceremony for the calmer morning or the still hour before sunset. No Spanish skipper licence is needed when you charter with a crew, and the operator confirms the exact requirements for your boat.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 1,200 to EUR 4,500 for a private wedding day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: typically 4 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: commonly 10 to 30 guests on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR (euros)
  • Departure: Puerto Banús, around 7 kilometres west of Marbella

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can you actually hold a wedding ceremony on a catamaran in Marbella?

Yes, many couples exchange vows aboard with a celebrant while the catamaran sits at anchor in a calm bay, then cruise back for drinks. A legally binding civil ceremony still needs a separate registry step ashore.

How many guests fit on a wedding catamaran here?

A 45 to 53 foot catamaran typically carries 20 to 30 guests, depending on the boat's licensed limit. Larger receptions are often split across two boats cruising together.

How far do wedding charters usually cruise?

A typical Marbella charter stays within a few nautical miles of the coast, with the run from Puerto Banús to the bays off Marbella covering roughly 4 miles. This keeps the water calm and the Sierra Blanca in view.

What is included in the price?

Most charters include the boat, fuel, the skipper and basic soft drinks, while catering, flowers and a celebrant are added separately. Always confirm the included hours and any extra cost for staying out past sunset.

To lock in a date for your Marbella catamaran wedding, set your guest numbers and check availability, then confirm the deposit with the operator; boat owners on the Costa del Sol can also list their catamaran to earn from wedding-season bookings. A short message secures the marina, the crew and the hours you need.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
